methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $12.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Thinking Machines Lab
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $12B

private.name: Sakana AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
FlexAI is a Seed-stage AI & Software company founded by Brijesh Tripathi and Dali Kilani that has raised €28.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies alongside one public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and eight private peers including Thinking Machines Lab, Sakana AI, OpenAI, and xAI.

The peer-median method anchors the primary valuation approach. The five selected comparables—Thinking Machines Lab ($12.0B, Seed), Liquid AI ($2.4B, Series A), Field AI ($2.0B, Series A), Sakana AI ($1.0B, Seed), and Twelve Labs ($250M, Series A)—span a wide range from $250M to $12.0B, with a median valuation of $2.0B. This method produces an implied valuation of $2.0B for FlexAI.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $1.7B to $2.3B, with a median point estimate of $2.0B. The wide peer range ($250M–$12.0B) reflects material stage and business-model variance within early-stage AI; this dispersion is a key valuation uncertainty. The limited application of a single triangulation method, combined with the heterogeneity of the comparable set, suggests the $1.7B–$2.3B range should be treated as directional rather than precise. Refinement would require additional valuation methods or tighter peer alignment on burn rate, revenue, and product maturity.
